name: Housing Consultants Group
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing HUD-aligned homebuyer education, financial wellness, and foreclosure prevention counseling to help individuals achieve sustainable homeownership.
description_long:
Housing Consultants Group (HCG) is a non-profit organization focused on guiding individuals and families toward generational wealth through education and counseling programs. The organization operates with a mission centered on financial stability and sustainable homeownership, positioning itself as an intermediary between prospective homebuyers and the lending/realtor community. HCG appears to serve the Greensboro and High Point, North Carolina areas based on references to local down payment assistance programs in those cities.

HCG offers a comprehensive suite of housing and financial counseling services designed for different stages of the homeownership journey. Their core offerings include Homebuyer Education Program (HBE) for those planning to purchase within 6-12 months, Financial Wellness Bootcamp for foundational credit and budgeting education, Individual Development Account (IDA) Program with monthly coaching, Foreclosure Prevention & Financial Sustainability Counseling for those in crisis, Post-Purchase Education for new homeowners, and specialized Lender/Realtor Training. Eligible program participants may also apply for local down payment assistance programs upon course completion.

HCG distinguishes itself through its structured, sequential approach to financial education tied to credit scores and demonstrated pathways. Rather than offering one-off counseling, they provide multi-month coaching relationships through the IDA program and tiered financial literacy classes. The organization has built an extensive partner network with lenders, realtors, and housing professionals, positioning itself as a connector that facilitates access to down payment assistance and sustainable lending. They also offer specialized training for lending and real estate professionals, not just consumers.

As a non-profit operating in the free-help category, HCG appears legitimate and mission-driven. However, the website provides limited detail on counselor credentials, NFCC certification status, HUD approval confirmation, or specific outcomes/success rates. Geographic scope seems limited to North Carolina areas, which may restrict accessibility. No pricing information, wait times, or client testimonials are visible on the homepage.
pros: Non-profit structure focused on generational wealth and sustainable homeownership, not profit extraction
Multi-stage program design (Bootcamp → IDA → Homebuyer Ed → Post-Purchase) addresses different financial starting points
Direct integration with down payment assistance programs for Greensboro and High Point, reducing barriers to purchase
Offers foreclosure prevention counseling during financial hardship, not just pre-purchase education
Extends services to lenders and realtors through training programs, creating systemic change beyond individual counseling
Individual Development Account (IDA) program includes monthly meetings and coaching, not one-time classes
Free registration available for services based on website language ('START REGISTRATION' listed without cost barriers)
cons: Geographic limitation: appears to operate primarily in Greensboro and High Point, NC; no indication of national or remote services
Limited transparency: website does not explicitly state NFCC certification, HUD approval, or counselor credential requirements
No outcome data visible: no client success rates, testimonials, or measurable results published on homepage
Unclear eligibility criteria: website does not specify income limits, credit score ranges, or who qualifies for each program
No pricing or timeline information: website doesn't disclose program lengths, session frequencies, or cost structures
best_for: First-time homebuyers in North Carolina with credit challenges or low savings seeking structured, long-term coaching
Homeowners facing foreclosure or financial hardship seeking counseling and crisis intervention resources
Individuals with minimal financial literacy wanting foundational budgeting and credit education before homebuying
Lenders and realtors seeking staff training on sustainable lending practices and down payment assistance programs
diagnosis: HCG is best for North Carolina residents seeking free, non-profit housing counseling and education aligned with the non-profit/HUD-certified counselor model. Primary caveat: verify HUD approval and NFCC certification directly, confirm geographic service area, and clarify program eligibility and timelines before enrollment, as these details are not visible on the public website.
services: Financial Wellness Bootcamp (credit and budgeting education)
Individual Development Account (IDA) Program with monthly coaching
Down payment assistance program application support
Interactive questionnaire to identify appropriate program fit
Housing counseling (general, implied by organization mission)
Financial literacy and credit score education classes
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
